title: Ornithogalum nanum
taxonomy: Ornithogalum nanum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae. It belongs to the genus Ornithogalum, which is a group of plants commonly known as starflowers. The genus Ornithogalum is part of the order Asparagales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within the Asparagaceae family, Ornithogalum nanum is also part of the subfamily Scilloideae. This classification places Ornithogalum nanum within a larger group of plants that are often found in dry, rocky areas.

side_effects: Ornithogalum nanum is known to be toxic if ingested. It contains toxic compounds called calcium oxalate crystals, which can cause oral and gastrointestinal irritation. If the plant sap comes into contact with the skin, it may cause dermatitis or skin irritation. Ingestion of the plant may also lead to nausea and vomiting. In severe cases, it can cause kidney damage if ingested in large quantities.
intro: Ornithogalum nanum, commonly known as the Star of Bethlehem, is a perennial plant in the Asparagaceae family, characterized by its white, bell-shaped flowers and strap-like leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, potentially beneficial for reducing pain and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Ornithogalum nanum include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including arthritis, wounds, and skin conditions.
